perturbation.original_text: MA shall keep accurate records of the sales of the Technology and Maintenance, including Client Registration Cards and shall make these records available for review by a representative of Company within ten (10) business days following the end of each month.
perturbation.changed_text: MA shall keep general records of the sales of the Technology and Maintenance, and these records will be available upon a reasonable request by Company, but not necessarily including detailed Client Registration Cards unless Company demands it in writing 30 days prior to auditing these records.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces uncertainty about the requirements for detailed record-keeping. The original text demands accurate records of sales including Client Registration Cards while the modified text introduces exceptions to the requirement and introduces a 30 days notice before demanding these records. This contradicts the need for ongoing monitoring and accurate sales records, creating uncertainty regarding enforcement of the sales record-keeping provisions. This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in section 3, and it contradicts requirements listed in section 7, Payment and Deliveries, as a well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MA shall keep general records of the sales of the Technology and Maintenance, and these records will be available upon a reasonable request by Company, but not necessarily including detailed Client Registration Cards unless Company demands it in writing 30 days prior to auditing these records.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the availability and detail of sales records. The original text mandates 'accurate records of the sales of the Technology and Maintenance, including Client Registration Cards' available within 'ten (10) business days'. The changed text qualifies this by stating 'general records...not necessarily including detailed Client Registration Cards unless Company demands it in writing 30 days prior to auditing'. This contradicts the original requirement for readily available and detailed records, introducing uncertainty and potentially hindering timely monitoring of sales activity. This uncertainty directly conflicts with the need for immediate and accurate record access as originally stipulated, creating an ambiguity about the obligation of MA to provide detailed records.

perturbation.original_text: Company shall supply MA access to all major published modifications or upgrades to the Technology, which add enhancements to or correct known errors in the Technology. Company shall provide Technology access to MA for each licensed customer so long as MA is not in default with any terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall endeavor to supply MA access to major modifications or upgrades to the Technology, but its availability is not guaranteed and depends on Company's internal resources and priorities. Company will attempt to provide Technology access to MA for each licensed customer, conditional to MA's fulfillment of some critical terms of this Agreement, as defined by Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the commitment to provide Technology upgrades and access. The original text mandates that Company 'shall' provide access, while the modified text weakens this obligation by stating Company 'shall endeavor' and makes the availability dependent on 'Company's internal resources'. This undermines the original committment that would allow the Marketing Affiliate to fulfill it's duties to distribute the software to it's clients. This change is made in section 8, and it's creating an in-text contradiction with that same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company shall endeavor to supply MA access to major modifications or upgrades to the Technology, but its availability is not guaranteed and depends on Company's internal resources and priorities. Company will attempt to provide Technology access to MA for each licensed customer, conditional to MA's fulfillment of some critical terms of this Agreement, as defined by Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the original commitment of providing technology upgrades and access. The original text states that Company 'shall' provide access, while the modified text uses 'shall endeavor' and makes the availability dependent on 'Company's internal resources'. The contradicted text reflects the changed text, reinforcing the weaker commitment. The changed text creates a contradiction because the original text mandated access, while the changed text makes it conditional and not guaranteed. Therefore, this creates an in-text contradiction, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.
